channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Xbox and Discord Team Up For Xbox Game Pass Starter Edition and Perks
item.content:encoded:
Following rumors and leaks of something of the sort coming, Microsoft and Discord today announced a new partnership between the companies which will bring new content to each of their subscriptions. This continues a trend of changes and tweaks that have come since Asha Sharmin took over including a price cut to Xbox Game Pass (although that left it more expensive than it was a year before still).

The clear headliner of the partnership is for Discord, and their premium Nitro subscription. As part of their new Nitro Rewards the subscription will include a special starter edition of Xbox Game Pass that will include over 50 PC and Console games. It will include 10 hours of cloud gaming and will get new games added periodically. There is not yet a complete list of games available with the service but it will include: Fallout 4, Stardew Valley, DayZ, Deep Rock Galactic, Overcooked 2, and Grounded, showing a focus on evergreen titles with multiplayer as well as first party Microsoft games. 

Additionally, Nitro Rewards will add discounts for Logitech G (30% off), SteelSeries (15% off), and KontrolFreek (20% off). Nitro Rewards are rolling out over all regions in the next few weeks where eligible. Nitro members will also get 250 Orbs each month, and a multiplier on orbs earned doing 'Quests'.

Xbox Game Pass Adds Discord Nitro Benefits

On a smaller note starting later this month, at no additional cost Xbox Game Pass subscribers can expect some Discord Nitro benefits. Microsoft doesn't make it clear which tier of Xbox Game Pass members will get these perks, though it may be all subscribers. The benefits will include 250 Discord Orbs each month, a 1.2x multiplier to Orbs earned when completing 'Quests', and some automatically applied discounts on the Discord shop.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
